title: Merge K Sorted Lists
problem_statement: Merge k sorted linked lists and return the merged sorted list.

examples.explanation: All sorted lists are merged into a single sorted list.

intuition: Use a min-heap to repeatedly extract the smallest current node from all lists.

approaches.name: Heap-based merge
approaches.description: Push the head of each list into a min-heap and repeatedly append the smallest node while pushing its successor.
approaches.time: O(N log k) where N is total nodes
approaches.why_not: Merging lists pairwise still works but can be slower than a single heap merge when k is large.

interview_qa.a: It stores the current smallest node from each list so the next merged value is always minimal.

interview_qa.q: How is the result list built?
interview_qa.a: By repeatedly polling the heap and attaching the smallest node to the merged output.
